From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Scott Thompson (comedian)](/w/index.php?title=Scott_Thompson_(comedian)&redirect=no "Scott Thompson (comedian)"))

Canadian comedian and actor (born 1959)

For the comedian also named Scott Thompson, see [Carrot Top](/wiki/Carrot_Top "Carrot Top").

Not to be confused with [Scott Thomson (actor)](/wiki/Scott_Thomson_(actor) "Scott Thomson (actor)").

| Scott Thompson | |
| --- | --- |
| Thompson in 2012 | |
| Born | John Scott Thompson   (1959-06-12) June 12, 1959 (age 66)  [North Bay, Ontario](/wiki/North_Bay,_Ontario "North Bay, Ontario"), Canada |
| Occupations | Actor, comedian |
| Years active | 1980–present |
| Website | [newscottlandland.com](http://newscottlandland.com) |

**John Scott Thompson** (born June 12, 1959) is a Canadian actor and comedian, best known as member of the comedy troupe [The Kids in the Hall](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall "The Kids in the Hall") and for playing Brian on *[The Larry Sanders Show](/wiki/The_Larry_Sanders_Show "The Larry Sanders Show")*.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Scott_Thompson_(actor)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Thompson was born in [North Bay, Ontario](/wiki/North_Bay,_Ontario "North Bay, Ontario"), and grew up in [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton,_Ontario "Brampton, Ontario"). Named for his uncle, he later dropped the name "John" to simplify his name for the stage. He is the second oldest of the five children in his family.

He attended [Brampton Centennial Secondary School](/wiki/Brampton_Centennial_Secondary_School "Brampton Centennial Secondary School"), and was a witness to the 1975 [Brampton Centennial Secondary School shooting](/wiki/Brampton_Centennial_Secondary_School_shooting "Brampton Centennial Secondary School shooting").[[1]](#cite_note-shooting-1) He enrolled at [York University](/wiki/York_University "York University") but in his third year was asked to leave for being "disruptive". He joined the comedy troupe The Love Cats, where he met [Mark McKinney](/wiki/Mark_McKinney "Mark McKinney").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Scott_Thompson_(actor)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/81/Scott_Thompson_at_Walk_of_Fame_unveiling.jpg/250px-Scott_Thompson_at_Walk_of_Fame_unveiling.jpg)](/wiki/File:Scott_Thompson_at_Walk_of_Fame_unveiling.jpg)

Thompson at his [Brampton Arts Walk of Fame](/wiki/Brampton_Arts_Walk_of_Fame "Brampton Arts Walk of Fame") star unveiling, 2014

In 1984, Thompson became a member of [The Kids in the Hall](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall "The Kids in the Hall"), whose [eponymous sketch comedy series](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(TV_series) "The Kids in the Hall (TV series)") aired starting 1989 on the [CBC](/wiki/Canadian_Broadcasting_Corporation "Canadian Broadcasting Corporation") in Canada and on [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") in the United States, but moved to [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") for its fourth and fifth seasons. Openly gay,[[2]](#cite_note-2) Thompson became best known on the show for his monologues as "alpha queen" socialite [Buddy Cole](/wiki/Buddy_Cole_(character) "Buddy Cole (character)"), and his appearances as [Queen Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II"), secretary Cathy, businessman Danny Husk, suburban housewife Fran, actress Francesca Fiore, and the demented old man in the popular "Love and Sausages" sketch.

Concurrently with *The Kids in the Hall*, Thompson and his writing colleague [Paul Bellini](/wiki/Paul_Bellini "Paul Bellini") collaborated in a [queercore](/wiki/Queercore "Queercore") punk band called Mouth Congress.[[3]](#cite_note-vanderbilt-3)

During the mid-1990s Thompson ran an interactive website, developed by his younger brother Craig and called ScottLand. It had a live-chat area, voting and comedy espionage and sold Buddy Cole T-shirts and video tapes of comedy sketches.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

He also appeared regularly on *[The Larry Sanders Show](/wiki/The_Larry_Sanders_Show "The Larry Sanders Show")* as Hank Kingsley's personal assistant Brian, and made numerous guest appearances on other television series, including *[Politically Incorrect](/wiki/Politically_Incorrect "Politically Incorrect")*, *[The Late Show](/wiki/Late_Show_with_David_Letterman "Late Show with David Letterman")*, *[Late Night with Conan O'Brien](/wiki/Late_Night_with_Conan_O%27Brien "Late Night with Conan O'Brien")*, and *[Train 48](/wiki/Train_48 "Train 48")*. Thompson hosted a [reality television](/wiki/Reality_television "Reality television") program in Canada called *[My Fabulous Gay Wedding](/wiki/My_Fabulous_Gay_Wedding "My Fabulous Gay Wedding")*. Thompson defended [Mordecai Richler](/wiki/Mordecai_Richler "Mordecai Richler")'s novel *[Cocksure](/wiki/Cocksure "Cocksure")* in *[Canada Reads](/wiki/Canada_Reads "Canada Reads") 2006*. He has continued to tour, and act in numerous movies and on TV. He joined the other Kids in the Hall to tour as recently as 2014, guest-starred in two episodes of *[Reno 911!](/wiki/Reno_911! "Reno 911!")*, and performed in the project *[Death Comes to Town](/wiki/Death_Comes_to_Town "Death Comes to Town")* (2010) with fellow KITH members [Dave Foley](/wiki/Dave_Foley "Dave Foley"), [Bruce McCulloch](/wiki/Bruce_McCulloch "Bruce McCulloch"), [Mark McKinney](/wiki/Mark_McKinney "Mark McKinney"), and [Kevin McDonald](/wiki/Kevin_McDonald "Kevin McDonald"). He had a recurring role in the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") series *[Hannibal](/wiki/Hannibal_(TV_series) "Hannibal (TV series)")*, playing Jimmy Price, an [FBI](/wiki/Federal_Bureau_of_Investigation "Federal Bureau of Investigation") crime scene investigator.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Thompson published a humour book, *Buddy Babylon: The Autobiography of Buddy Cole*, and a graphic novel, *The Hollow Planet*, based on characters from *The Kids in the Hall*,[[6]](#cite_note-6) and has written and performed two one-man shows. In 2014, Thompson, in character as Buddy Cole, did a series of reports on *[The Colbert Report](/wiki/The_Colbert_Report "The Colbert Report")* as the program's correspondent for the [2014 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2014_Winter_Olympics "2014 Winter Olympics").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 2015, Bellini and Thompson uploaded all of their Mouth Congress recordings to [Bandcamp](/wiki/Bandcamp "Bandcamp"),[[3]](#cite_note-vanderbilt-3) and they reunited the following year for several live shows to promote the release.[[8]](#cite_note-8) They launched a [Kickstarter](/wiki/Kickstarter "Kickstarter") campaign to fund a documentary film about the band; that film, *[Mouth Congress](/wiki/Mouth_Congress "Mouth Congress")*, premiered at the [Kingston Canadian Film Festival](/wiki/Kingston_Canadian_Film_Festival "Kingston Canadian Film Festival") in 2021.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Around this time, Thompson performed his Buddy Cole monologues at the [Portland Queer Comedy Festival](/wiki/Portland_Queer_Comedy_Festival "Portland Queer Comedy Festival").[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In 2018, Thompson launched *Après le Déluge – The Buddy Cole Monologues*, a one-man show in character as Buddy Cole.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Scott_Thompson_(actor)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

### Firebombing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Scott_Thompson_(actor)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Firebombing")]

In 2000, Thompson was living with his boyfriend, French documentarian Joel Soler, in [Hollywood](/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles"). Soler had smuggled footage out of Iraq to make an [E!](/wiki/E! "E!") News-style satiric political documentary comedy, *Uncle Saddam*, about the eccentricities in the home life of [Saddam Hussein](/wiki/Saddam_Hussein "Saddam Hussein") and his family, which bubbled behind Hussein's dictatorial façade. Thompson wrote the narration for the movie, which was read by actor [Wallace Langham](/wiki/Wallace_Langham "Wallace Langham"). Following the movie's release, Thompson and Soler's [West Hollywood](/wiki/West_Hollywood "West Hollywood") home was firebombed on November 1, 2000. Thompson has discussed the details of this incident in interviews with [Jesse Brown](/wiki/Jesse_Brown_(journalist) "Jesse Brown (journalist)") of *[Canadaland](/wiki/Canadaland "Canadaland")* and fellow Canadian comic [Elvira Kurt](/wiki/Elvira_Kurt "Elvira Kurt"), as being inspiration for his future show *The Lowest Show on Earth*. In the interview with Kurt, he says of the attack, "We were sleeping and a group came to our home. They filled our giant garbage cans with gasoline and set them on fire on our front lawn. They had buckets of red paint. They covered the house with it so it dripped off like blood. They put a note in the front hall that said, 'In the name of Allah, the merciful and compassionate, burn this Satanic film or you will be dead'. They underlined "dead" just in case we weren't freaked out enough".[[14]](#cite_note-14)

This, along with many other incidents throughout Thompson's life, including the 1975 [Brampton Centennial Secondary School shooting](/wiki/Brampton_Centennial_Secondary_School_shooting "Brampton Centennial Secondary School shooting") at his Brampton high school, led him to process incidents of terror on micro- and macrocosmic levels through his one-man comedy show *The Lowest Show on Earth*. Thompson went on tour with this show and secured a spot in New York, off-Broadway. The posters—featuring Thompson lying [supine](/wiki/Supine_position "Supine position") on the ground with a big wad of [semen](/wiki/Semen "Semen") dripping down the side of his face—went up around the city on September 10, 2001. The following day, the [terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center](/wiki/September_11_attacks "September 11 attacks") made the one-man show's difficult material impossible to talk about.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Scott_Thompson_(actor)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Health")]

In March 2009, Thompson was diagnosed with [B-cell](/wiki/B-cell "B-cell") [non-Hodgkin's](/wiki/Non-Hodgkin%27s_lymphoma "Non-Hodgkin's lymphoma") [gastric lymphoma](/wiki/Gastric_lymphoma "Gastric lymphoma"). He completed six rounds of [chemotherapy](/wiki/Chemotherapy "Chemotherapy") and one month of radiation and reached a cancer-free status.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)

## Other works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Scott_Thompson_(actor)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Other works")]

* *[Buddy Babylon: The Autobiography of Buddy Cole](/wiki/Buddy_Babylon:_The_Autobiography_of_Buddy_Cole "Buddy Babylon: The Autobiography of Buddy Cole")* (with [Paul Bellini](/wiki/Paul_Bellini "Paul Bellini")) in 1998, a humour novel; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-440-50828-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-440-50828-2 "Special:BookSources/0-440-50828-2")
* *The Lowest Show on Earth*, a 2001 one-man show produced in [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto")
* *Scottastrophe*, 2006 multimedia show
* *The Hollow Planet*, a graphic novel by Scott Thompson, Kyle Morton, and Stephan Nilson, from [IDW Publishing](/wiki/IDW_Publishing "IDW Publishing"); featuring Thompson's *[The Kids in the Hall](/wiki/The_Kids_in_the_Hall_(TV_series) "The Kids in the Hall (TV series)")* character Danny Husk.
